transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 57 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 66 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 69 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 70 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1305 of file websocket/transport.c.

1306                                                                                        {
1307   if (!name) {
1308     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1309     return NULL;
1310   }
1311 
1312   if (!url) {
1313     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1314     return NULL;
1315   }
1316 
1317   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1318   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1319   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1320   if (!protocol_end) {
1321     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1322     return NULL;
1323   }
1324 
1325   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1326   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1327 
1328   // Find port (if specified)
1329   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1330   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1331 
1332   char host[256] = {0};
1333   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1334   char path[256] = "/";
1335 
1336   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1337     // Port is specified
1338     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1339     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1340       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1341       return NULL;
1342     }
1343     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1344     host[host_len] = '\0';
1345 
1346     // Extract port
1347     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1348     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1349       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1350       return NULL;
1351     }
1352   } else {
1353     // No port specified, use default
1354     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1355     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1356       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1357       return NULL;
1358     }
1359     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1360     host[host_len] = '\0';
1361   }
1362 
1363   // Extract path
1364   if (path_start) {
1365     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1366     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1367   }
1368 
1369   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1370 
1371   // Allocate transport structure
1372   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1373   if (!transport) {
1374     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1375     return NULL;
1376   }
1377 
1378   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1379   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1380       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1381   if (!ws_data) {
1382     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1383     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1384     return NULL;
1385   }
1386 
1387   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1388   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1389 
1390   // Create receive queue
1391   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1392   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1393     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1394     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1395     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1396     return NULL;
1397   }
1398 
1399   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1400   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1401   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1402     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1403     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1404     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1405     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1406     return NULL;
1407   }
1408 
1409   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1410   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1411   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1412   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1413 
1414   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1415     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1416     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1417     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1418     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1419     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1420     return NULL;
1421   }
1422 
1423   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1424     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1425     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1426     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1427     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1428     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1429     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1430     return NULL;
1431   }
1432 
1433   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1434     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1435     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1436     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1437     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1438     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1439     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1440     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1441     return NULL;
1442   }
1443 
1444   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1445     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1446     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1447     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1448     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1449     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1450     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1451     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1452     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1453     return NULL;
1454   }
1455 
1456   // Allocate initial send buffer
1457   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1458   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1459   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1460     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1461     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1462     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1463     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1464     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1465     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1466     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1467     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1468     return NULL;
1469   }
1470 
1471   // Create libwebsockets context
1472   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1473   // IMPORTANT: "http" protocol MUST be first for WebSocket upgrade handshake to work
1474   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1475       {
1476           "http", // Required first for WebSocket upgrade
1477           websocket_callback,
1478           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1479           524288, // RX buffer size
1480           0,      // ID
1481           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1482           524288  // TX packet size
1483       },
1484       {
1485           "acip", // ACIP protocol
1486           websocket_callback,
1487           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1488           524288, // RX buffer size
1489           0,      // ID
1490           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1491           524288  // TX packet size
1492       },
1493       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1494   };
1495 
1496   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1497   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1498   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1499   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1500   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1501   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1502   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1503 
1504   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1505   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1506   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1507   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1508   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1509   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1510   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1511   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1512 
1513   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1514   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1515   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1516   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1517 
1518   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1519   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1520   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1521       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1522       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1523   };
1524   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1525 
1526   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1527   lws_log_init_client();
1528 
1529   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1530 
1531   if (!ws_data->context) {
1532     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1533     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1534     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1535     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1536     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1537     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1538     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1539     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1540     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1541     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1542     return NULL;
1543   }
1544 
1545   // Connect to WebSocket server
1546   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1547   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1548   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1549   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1550   connect_info.address = host;
1551   connect_info.port = port;
1552   connect_info.path = path;
1553   connect_info.host = host;
1554   connect_info.origin = host;
1555   connect_info.protocol = "acip";
1556   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1557   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1558   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1559 
1560   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1561   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1562   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1563   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1564     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1565     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1566     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1567     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1568     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1569     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1570     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1571     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1572     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1573     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1574     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1575     return NULL;
1576   }
1577 
1578   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1579   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1580   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1581 
1582   // Initialize transport
1583   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1584   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1585   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1586 
1587   // CRITICAL: Give libwebsockets time to initialize connection before starting service thread
1588   // libwebsockets needs to process the initial connection handshake callbacks before lws_service()
1589   // is called repeatedly. Concurrent access to context during connection setup can trigger assertions.
1590   // Sleep 50ms to let lws_client_connect_via_info() callbacks complete initialization.
1591   log_debug("Delaying service thread start to allow libwebsockets connection initialization...");
1592   platform_sleep_us(50000); // 50ms delay
1593 
1594   // Start service thread after connection initialization
1595   // Only the service thread should call lws_service() on this context
1596   log_debug("Starting WebSocket service thread...");
1597   ws_data->service_running = true;
1598   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1599     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1600     ws_data->service_running = false;
1601     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1602     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1603     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1604     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1605     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1606     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1607     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1608     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1609     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1610     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1611     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1612     return NULL;
1613   }
1614   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1615 
1616   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1617   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1618   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1619   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1620   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1621   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1622 
1623   // Register transport
1624   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1625 
1626   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1627   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1628 
1629   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1630   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1631   return transport;
1632 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), platform_sleep_us(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1644 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1645                                                                                        {
1646   if (!name) {
1647     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1648     return NULL;
1649   }
1650 
1651   if (!wsi) {
1652     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1653     return NULL;
1654   }
1655 
1656   // Allocate transport structure
1657   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1658   if (!transport) {
1659     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1660     return NULL;
1661   }
1662 
1663   // Allocate transport-specific data
1664   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1665       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1666   if (!ws_data) {
1667     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1668     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1669     return NULL;
1670   }
1671 
1672   // Initialize receive queue
1673   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1674   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1675     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1676     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1677     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1678     return NULL;
1679   }
1680 
1681   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1682   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1683   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1684     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1685     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1686     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1687     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1688     return NULL;
1689   }
1690 
1691   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1692   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1693   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1694   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1695   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1696     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1697     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1698     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1699     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1700     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1701     return NULL;
1702   }
1703 
1704   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1705   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1706   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1707   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1708   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1709   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1710 
1711   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1712     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1713     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1714     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1715     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1716     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1717     return NULL;
1718   }
1719 
1720   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1721     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1722     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1723     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1724     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1725     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1726     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1727     return NULL;
1728   }
1729 
1730   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1731     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1732     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1733     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1734     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1735     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1736     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1737     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1738     return NULL;
1739   }
1740 
1741   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1742     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1743     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1744     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1745     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1746     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1747     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1748     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1749     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1750     return NULL;
1751   }
1752 
1753   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1754     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1755     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1756     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1757     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1758     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1759     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1760     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1761     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1762     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1763     return NULL;
1764   }
1765 
1766   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1767     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1768     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1769     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1770     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1771     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1772     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1773     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1774     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1775     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1776     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1777     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1778     return NULL;
1779   }
1780 
1781   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1782   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1783   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1784   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1785     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1786     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1787     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1788     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1789     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1790     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1791     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1792     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1793     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1794     return NULL;
1795   }
1796   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1797 
1798   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1799   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1800   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1801   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1802   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1803   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1804            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1805   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1806 
1807   // Initialize transport
1808   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1809   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1810   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1811 
1812   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1813 
1814   // Register transport
1815   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1816 
1817   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1818   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1819 
1820   return transport;
1821 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
